purpose: Freeze a strong EnOpt-style nonlinear classical baseline before any fresh-validation docking score is available.

relationship_to_primary_gate.rationale: The 2024 EnOpt study makes a score-matrix tree model a necessary comparator for a receptor-subset optimization claim. This baseline cannot rescue or invalidate the separately preregistered QUBO endpoint.

data_boundary.training_data: hashed Train-696 matrices and labels only
data_boundary.fresh_validation_panel: must not be opened by the fitting implementation
data_boundary.fresh_validation_scores: unavailable and prohibited from fitting, feature selection, hyperparameter selection, or calibration
forbidden_actions: read fresh-validation or test rows in the fitting implementation
change receptor subsets or hyperparameters after validation scores are available
fit a chemical-descriptor model that can bypass the receptor score matrix
change the existing QUBO validation acceptance criteria
describe a supplementary XGBoost result as quantum advantage

cross_validation.fold_definition: reuse the same deterministic grouped fold builder and seed as the final Train-696 QUBO gate; each outer fit tunes on the other three folds only
cross_validation.normalization: fit independent per-receptor min-max bounds on the current training fold only; apply without clipping
selection_tie_breakers: deterministic parameter and receptor lexical order

xgboost.model_input: normalized receptor docking scores only; no ligand descriptors

methods.xgboost_all5: use all five receptors required by the frozen validation comparator union
methods.xgboost_budget3: select one of all ten five-choose-three receptor subsets strictly inside each training CV context

future_validation.timing: run only after the primary fresh-validation evaluator in the same frozen evaluation event
future_validation.matrix_application: apply the frozen model separately to primary, sensitivity, seed0, seed1, and seed2 matrices using the corresponding frozen Train-696 normalization bounds
future_validation.decision_role: descriptive strong classical comparison; no change to the preregistered QUBO pass/fail result

stopping_rule: Fit once, repeat once only for deterministic hash verification, freeze the resulting models, and perform no further XGBoost tuning before fresh validation.
interpretation_boundary: Nested Train-696 OOF performance estimates a classical nonlinear baseline but is not independent validation. The frozen models are supplementary comparators and cannot change the existing QUBO acceptance endpoint. Neither a stronger nor weaker XGBoost result establishes affinity, activity, cross-target generalization, quantum computation, or quantum advantage.
